l1_idioms.example: She always goes the extra mile to help her team.
I really look up to my older brother.
My grandmother has a heart of gold.

l2_idioms.example: He decided to follow in his father's footsteps and become a doctor.

l2_idioms.idiom: The apple doesn't fall far from the tree
l2_idioms.example: He is stubborn just like his dad; the apple doesn't fall far from the tree.
You cannot treat your own flesh and blood like that.

l1_vocab.meaning: 世界性的 / 国际化的
利润丰厚的 / 赚钱的

l1_idioms.example: She experienced severe culture shock when she moved to Tokyo.
Traveling abroad is a great way to broaden your horizons.

l1_idioms.meaning: 白手起家 / 从头开始
l1_idioms.example: After the fire, they had to rebuild their business and start from scratch.

l2_idioms.example: New York City has always been a cultural melting pot.

l2_idioms.idiom: When in Rome, do as the Romans do
l2_idioms.example: I don't usually eat spicy food, but when in Rome, do as the Romans do.
They used a translation app to overcome the language barrier.

l1_idioms.example: I worked hard to climb the career ladder from intern to manager.
Being a pilot has always been my dream job.
It is difficult to make a living as an artist.

l2_idioms.example: In many families, both parents work to bring home the bacon.
He quit because he felt stuck in a dead-end job.
To solve this economic crisis, we must think outside the box.

l1_idioms.example: The sequel was a huge let-down compared to the first movie.
The media hyped up the film, but it was actually boring.
Watching that movie was a complete waste of time.

l2_vocab.meaning: 自动化 (used in context of CGI)

l2_idioms.example: The new Marvel movie is a guaranteed box office hit.
The film features a star-studded cast of famous actors.

l2_idioms.idiom: On the edge of one's seat
l2_idioms.meaning: 由于紧张兴奋而坐立不安
l2_idioms.example: The thriller kept the audience on the edge of their seats.

l1_idioms.example: We should all do our bit to recycle plastic.
He talks about saving energy, but he doesn't practice what he preaches.
As a nature lover, she spends every weekend hiking.

l2_idioms.example: One person recycling is just a drop in the ocean, but it helps.
Plastic pollution is just the tip of the iceberg of environmental issues.
The storm wreaked havoc on the coastal ecosystem.

l1_idioms.example: I found a beautiful park that was off the beaten track.
I go to the forest to enjoy some peace and quiet.
Camping is a great way to get away from it all.

l2_vocab.meaning: 有治疗作用的 / 治愈的

l2_idioms.example: Living in a concrete jungle can be stressful without green spaces.

l2_idioms.meaning: 令人耳目一新的人或事
l2_idioms.example: This new park design is a breath of fresh air for the city.
Many city dwellers want to get back to nature on weekends.

l1_vocab.meaning: 用户友好的 / 易用的

l1_idioms.example: My little brother is constantly glued to the screen.
With this app, all the information is at your fingertips.
I often lose track of time when playing games.

l2_idioms.example: Technology is a double-edged sword; it connects and distracts us.
Social media often causes information overload.
She is a social butterfly online but shy in real life.

l1_idioms.example: We are both unemployed, so we are in the same boat.
Don't take his criticism to heart; he was just tired.
My grandfather gave me a word of wisdom about patience.

l2_idioms.example: You can't sit on the fence forever; you must make a decision.

l2_idioms.meaning: 问道于盲 (外行指导外行)
l2_idioms.example: Asking him for advice is like the blind leading the blind.
He didn't study and learned the hard way when he failed.

l1_idioms.example: The child thinks outside the box when painting.

l1_idioms.meaning: 如鱼得水 (在自己擅长的领域)
l1_idioms.example: She is truly in her element when drawing.
He has a hidden talent for sculpture.

l2_idioms.idiom: Beauty is in the eye of the beholder
l2_idioms.meaning: 情人眼里出西施 (审美因人而异)
l2_idioms.example: Modern art proves that beauty is in the eye of the beholder.
Despite his fame, the artist is very down to earth.
The report paints a bleak picture of the funding crisis.

l1_vocab.meaning: 破旧的 / 年久失修的

l1_idioms.example: The Eiffel Tower is a famous landmark.
This ancient castle has stood the test of time.
The view from the skyscraper took my breath away.

l2_idioms.example: Skyscrapers turn the city into a concrete jungle.
Everyone deserves a roof over their head.
Architecture can build bridges between cultures.

l1_idioms.example: This new smartphone is an absolute must-have.
The hospital uses state-of-the-art medical equipment.

l1_idioms.idiom: Cost an arm and a leg
l1_idioms.example: That luxury watch cost an arm and a leg.

l2_idioms.meaning: 一线希望 / 坏事中的好事
l2_idioms.example: Every cloud has a silver lining, even this mistake.

l2_idioms.idiom: At the click of a button
l2_idioms.meaning: 弹指之间 / 极其便捷
l2_idioms.example: You can order dinner at the click of a button.
We must upgrade our software to keep up with the times.

l1_idioms.meaning: 原地打转 / 毫无进展
l1_idioms.example: We were completely lost and just went around in circles.

l1_idioms.meaning: 注定失败的事 / 没救了
l1_idioms.example: Trying to fix this broken compass is a lost cause.
We finally arrived home safe and sound.

l2_idioms.meaning: 适应新环境 / 站稳脚跟
l2_idioms.example: It took him a few months to find his feet in the new city.
The little cafe is entirely off the beaten track.

l2_idioms.idiom: Cross that bridge when you come to it
l2_idioms.example: Don't worry about the final exam yet; we will cross that bridge when we come to it.

l1_idioms.example: Despite his enormous success, he remains very down to earth.
My high school teacher was a great role model for me.

l1_idioms.meaning: 处于聚光灯下 / 受人关注
l1_idioms.example: The young actor loves being in the limelight.

l2_idioms.example: Losing her privacy was just the price of fame.
That brand quickly became a household name.
Celebrities often feel like they live in a fishbowl.

l1_idioms.example: On Black Friday, we always shop till we drop.
I don't have money, so I am just window shopping.
She is a keen bargain hunter who loves sales.

l2_idioms.example: I felt sad, so I engaged in some retail therapy.

l2_idioms.meaning: 花费太高 / 倾家荡产
l2_idioms.example: Buying that fancy dress will definitely break the bank.
The bright neon sign caught my eye.

l1_idioms.example: Her latest thriller is a real page-turner.
I can easily lose myself in a good science fiction novel.

l1_idioms.idiom: Judge a book by its cover
l1_idioms.example: He looks rough, but never judge a book by its cover.

l2_idioms.example: You have to read between the lines to understand his true message.
My sister is a total bookworm and reads daily.
I have an exam tomorrow, so I need to hit the books.

l1_idioms.example: The whole class burst into laughter at his joke.
He loves to crack a joke during serious meetings.
They say laughter is the best medicine for sadness.

l2_idioms.idiom: Wear one's heart on one's sleeve
l2_idioms.example: She always wears her heart on her sleeve.
I couldn't keep a straight face when he fell.
He has a great sense of humor.

l1_idioms.example: We have been best friends through thick and thin.
They hit it off as soon as they met.
Over the years, the two friends began to drift apart.

l2_idioms.example: I realized he was just a fair-weather friend when I lost my job.
We don't always see eye to eye on politics.

l2_idioms.idiom: A friend in need is a friend indeed
l2_idioms.example: She helped me when I was broke; a friend in need is a friend indeed.

l1_idioms.cn_idiom: 金窝银窝不如自己的狗窝
l1_idioms.example: It feels so good to be back, home sweet home.

l1_idioms.meaning: 不拘束 / 像在自己家一样
l1_idioms.example: Please come in, sit down, and make yourself at home.
I moved to the countryside for some peace and quiet.

l2_idioms.example: It is difficult for young people to get on the property ladder.
House prices in this city have gone through the roof.
Everyone deserves to have a roof over their head.

l1_idioms.example: Hearing that old song on the radio was a blast from the past.
She holds her grandmother's necklace dear.
This recipe has been passed down for generations.

l2_idioms.idiom: One man's trash is another man's treasure
l2_idioms.example: I found this beautiful lamp at a yard sale; one man's trash is another man's treasure.
My uncle is a hoarder who keeps every old newspaper.
We live in a throwaway society where nothing is repaired.

l1_idioms.example: We need to hit the road early tomorrow morning.
I prefer to travel light and only take a backpack.
The project was a bumpy ride, but we finished it.

l2_idioms.example: We should reduce our carbon footprint by flying less.
She moved to escape the hustle and bustle of the city.
Traffic is terrible during the morning rush hour.

l1_idioms.meaning: 含着金汤匙出生 (context of luck vs talent)
l1_idioms.example: He didn't have to work hard because he was born with a silver spoon.
A good debater must be able to think on their feet.
She had never skied before, but she proved to be a natural.

l2_idioms.example: Don't give up on the guitar; practice makes perfect.
My younger sister is very brainy and always gets top marks.
He is a jack of all trades who can fix almost anything in the house.

l1_idioms.example: She is the one who really runs the show in this department.
The retiring CEO is ready to pass the torch to his successor.
We prefer buying our groceries from the local mom and pop store.

l2_idioms.example: The advertising industry can be extremely cut-throat.
Many small shops went out of business during the pandemic.
In the corporate world, big fish eat little fish.

l1_idioms.meaning: 小儿科 / 极容易的事
l1_idioms.example: For an expert like him, fixing this engine is child's play.
Looking through old photo albums was a real trip down memory lane.

l1_idioms.meaning: 掌上明珠 / 最得意的珍宝
l1_idioms.example: His newly restored vintage car is his pride and joy.

l2_idioms.example: She acts like a spoiled brat when she doesn't get her way.
You cannot wrap your children in cotton wool forever.
It is important that everyone learns to play by the rules.

l1_idioms.meaning: 蒙在鼓里 / 在黑暗中
l1_idioms.example: They kept me in the dark about the surprise party.
The vending machine is currently out of order.
Her quick thinking really saved the day.

l2_idioms.example: Environmentalists are fighting against the throwaway culture.
The carpet is showing signs of normal wear and tear.

l2_idioms.meaning: 没坏就别修 (多一事不如少一事)
l2_idioms.example: The system works fine, so don't fix what isn't broken.

l1_idioms.example: I managed to talk him into joining our gym.
I have never played tennis, but I'll give it a shot.

l1_idioms.idiom: Two heads are better than one
l1_idioms.meaning: 三个臭皮匠顶个诸葛亮
l1_idioms.example: Let's solve this puzzle together; two heads are better than one.

l2_idioms.example: He only started drinking because of peer pressure.
A truly great manager knows how to lead by example.
I always welcome constructive criticism to improve my work.

l1_vocab.meaning: 无懈可击的 / 完美的

l1_idioms.meaning: 超越期望 / 尽心尽力
l1_idioms.example: The hotel staff really go above and beyond for their guests.
He always serves his customers with a smile.
My grandparents always treat me like royalty when I visit.

l2_idioms.example: In the retail business, they say the customer is always right.
Automated messages can never replace the human touch.
This budget smartphone gives you a lot of bang for your buck.

l1_vocab.meaning: 病毒式传播的 / 爆火的

l1_idioms.example: We decided to binge-watch the entire season on Sunday.
I waste too much time scrolling through social media.
Her comedy video blew up overnight.

l2_idioms.example: You must be careful not to share fake news.
His funny cat video went viral within hours.
He is such a couch potato on the weekends.

l1_idioms.example: He told a funny story to break the ice at the party.
My cousin is always the life of the party.
We really had a blast at the music festival.

l2_idioms.example: It's Friday night, time to let your hair down.
I try to spend quality time with my children every evening.
Of course you can invite your friends; the more the merrier!

l1_idioms.cn_idiom: 忍气吞声 / 放下脸面
l1_idioms.example: He had to swallow his pride and ask for a loan.
We had a long talk to clear the air.

l1_idioms.meaning: 吐露心声 / 一吐为快
l1_idioms.example: I really need to get this secret off my chest.

l2_idioms.example: I told a white lie so I wouldn't ruin the surprise.
Just tell her the truth; honesty is the best policy.
After fighting for years, the brothers finally buried the hatchet.

l1_idioms.example: We finally met face to face after years of calling.
Pandas are rarely seen in the wild anymore.
The lion is widely known as the king of the jungle.

l2_idioms.example: In nature, it is all about the survival of the fittest.
He didn't really care; he was just shedding crocodile tears.

l2_idioms.meaning: 莫惹是非 / 别自找麻烦
l2_idioms.example: I didn't bring up the old argument because I wanted to let sleeping dogs lie.

l1_idioms.meaning: 高深学问 (usually negative: not rocket science)
l1_idioms.example: Just read the instructions; assembling this desk isn't rocket science.

l1_idioms.cn_idiom: 精神食粮 / 耐人寻味
l1_idioms.example: The professor's lecture gave us a lot of food for thought.
I had a light bulb moment and suddenly knew how to solve the math problem.

l2_idioms.meaning: 做无用功 / 重复发明
l2_idioms.example: There is already a good tool for this, so don't reinvent the wheel.
The salesman tried to blind us with science.
We both bought the exact same gift; great minds think alike.

l1_idioms.example: I've never cooked Thai food, but I'll give it a go.
This video editing software has a steep learning curve.
Driving a manual car is hard at first, but you'll soon get the hang of it.

l2_idioms.example: Keep practicing your piano scales; practice makes perfect.
It will take a few weeks to learn the ropes at your new job.
Learning a new language will broaden your horizons.

l1_idioms.meaning: 改过自新 / 重新开始
l1_idioms.example: He turned over a new leaf and stopped smoking.
I'm trying to kick the habit of eating junk food late at night.
My dad is a creature of habit who eats the exact same breakfast every day.

l2_idioms.example: I still bite my nails when I'm nervous; old habits die hard.

l2_idioms.meaning: 固执己见 / 墨守成规
l2_idioms.example: He is too set in his ways to use a modern smartphone.
My mother is an early bird who wakes up at 5 AM.

l1_idioms.example: If you want to play the game, you must follow the rules.
The teacher bent the rules to give him an extra day for the assignment.
Please keep your voice down while the baby is sleeping.

l2_idioms.example: The military was brought in to restore law and order.
Your joke about his family really crossed the line.
Saying please and thank you is just common courtesy.

l1_idioms.example: We played mobile games to kill time while waiting for the train.
If you want to save money on flights, you need to plan ahead.
The line was long, but the delicious food was definitely worth the wait.

l2_idioms.example: Time flies when you are having fun with friends.

l2_idioms.meaning: 亡羊补牢 / 迟做总比不做好
l2_idioms.example: He finally submitted his homework; well, better late than never.
They managed to beat the clock and finish the exam right before the bell.

l1_vocab.meaning: 好胜的 / 竞争激烈的

l1_idioms.example: Our team was so well prepared that we won hands down.

l1_idioms.idiom: Be in it to win it
l1_idioms.example: She trained every day because she is in it to win it.
Go out on that field and give it your all.

l2_idioms.example: The new regulations ensure a level playing field for all teams.

l2_idioms.idiom: Keep one's eye on the ball
l2_idioms.example: In business, you must keep your eye on the ball to succeed.
This trade agreement is a win-win situation for both countries.

l1_idioms.example: Could you lend a hand moving this heavy sofa?
Please keep an eye on my dog while I go inside the store.
My next-door neighbor baked me some cookies yesterday.

l2_idioms.meaning: 保持距离才能和睦相处
l2_idioms.example: We respect their privacy because good fences make good neighbors.
In this small village, everyone looks out for each other.
That sad movie hit a little too close to home.

l1_idioms.example: Our hotel is just a stone's throw away from the beach.
We spent the entire afternoon soaking up the sun.

l1_idioms.meaning: 清澈见底 / 一清二楚
l1_idioms.example: The ocean water in the Maldives is crystal clear.

l2_idioms.example: My $10 donation is just a drop in the ocean, but I want to help.

l2_idioms.meaning: 兴风作浪 / 引起轰动
l2_idioms.example: The young politician is already making waves in the capital.
The coach's speech at halftime really helped turn the tide.

l1_idioms.example: She walked into the party dressed to kill.
This tailored suit fits like a glove.
As a popular singer, she is a true fashion trendsetter.

l2_idioms.example: He wears expensive but ugly clothes; he is a complete fashion victim.

l2_idioms.idiom: Judge a book by its cover
l2_idioms.example: She dressed plainly, but she was a genius; never judge a book by its cover.
She buys a new wardrobe every season; she is a slave to fashion.

l1_idioms.example: After passing our exams, we went out to paint the town red.
The kids had a ball at the amusement park.
Bring your whole family to the dinner; the more the merrier.

l2_idioms.example: We bake these cookies every year to keep the tradition alive.
This neighborhood is a melting pot of different cultures.
Everyone at the wedding was in high spirits.
